title: haloragis erecta subsp. cartilaginea
intro: Haloragis erecta subsp. cartilaginea, commonly known as the cartilaginous watermilfoil or water milfoil, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Haloragaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal problems.
